front: Poem: opens "Huffy Henry hid the day, / unappeasable Henry sulked" — collection of 385 three-stanza, eighteen-line poems
Poem: entry 14 declares "Life, friends, is boring. We must not say so" — speaker concludes he has no "Inner Resources"
Poem: speaker describes father who "rose with his gun and went outdoors by my window / and did what was needed" and spits on "this dreadful banker's grave"
Poem: features conversations between alter ego Henry and his friend Mr. Bones
Poem: expanded in His Toy, His Dream, His Rest
Poem: speaker calls himself "the king of the pool," "the little man who smokes and smokes," and "the blackt-out man"
Poem: contains elegy for William Carlos Williams as a "lovely man" who "delivered infinite babies, in one great birth"
Poem: concludes "Nobody is ever missing" — all four season finale episode titles of Succession drawn from entry 29
Poem: includes plea to "save us from shotguns & fathers' suicides" and calls life "a handkerchief sandwich"
Poem: a woman "fills her compact and delicious body / with Chicken Paprika" — she "might as well be on Mars"
Poem: addressed to the author of The Tenth Muse Lately Sprung Up in America
Poem: asks "moved you not, restless, waiting for [your husband]?"
Poem: concludes with "Hover, utter, still, a sourcing whom my lost candle like the firefly loves"; illustrated by Ben Shahn
Author: committed suicide by jumping off the Washington Avenue Bridge in Minneapolis
Author: birth name was John Allyn Smith Jr.
Author: claimed "Death is a German expert" in a poem about a boy who dropped his ball learning "the epistemology of loss"
Author: published a critical study of Stephen Crane in 1950
